objects.name: Banking Organization (TTX participant)
objects.description: Private banking institution participating in the tabletop exercise.

objects.name: Departing insider with client-book access
objects.description: Employee abuses legitimate access to exfiltrate sensitive client data before departure.

objects.name: Unapproved external storage device
objects.description: External storage media used to copy client-book data outside authorized channels.

objects.name: Exfiltrate customer data using legitimate access
objects.description: Insider exports or copies customer information from authorized systems for unauthorized use.

objects.name: Insider theft of high-net-worth customer data
objects.description: Simulated insider incident involving unauthorized copying of private banking client records.

objects.content: Monitoring shows a relationship manager exported an unusually large volume of high-net-worth client records in the final week before resignation.

objects.abstract: Inject 2: legal hold and client risk decision
objects.content: The bank must decide whether to suspend departure processing, preserve devices, notify affected clients, and coordinate law-enforcement or civil action.

objects.name: Insider-risk alerting for abnormal customer-data exports
objects.description: Alert on unusual export volume, sensitive client-book access, and offboarding-period activity anomalies.

objects.name: Cross-functional insider case management
objects.description: Coordinate security, legal, HR, and client teams for evidence handling and response decisions.

objects.content: A preliminary review of messages and case notes indicates the employee has accepted a role with a competitor and may have intended to use the copied client-book data after departure. There is no evidence yet that the data has been publicly posted or sold.

objects.abstract: Inject 4: decision point - contain, notify, escalate
objects.content: The bank must decide whether to suspend departure processing, preserve devices, notify affected clients, and coordinate law-enforcement or civil action. Investigators estimate that several hundred high-net-worth client records may have been exposed, including contact details, account relationship details, and portfolio-related information.

The employee's access has been revoked, devices preserved, and the immediate threat contained. The organization now needs to determine final notifications, pursue legal remedies, monitor for downstream misuse, and document lessons learned.

objects.name: TTX Scenario 5 - Insider theft of high-net-worth client data
objects.description: Scenario tests insider-risk detection, legal/HR coordination, and sensitive client breach handling.
